mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >50% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and a contraction in housing starts indicates demand destruction outpacing supply adjustments, which compresses profit margins and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive Rank IC > 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 102 signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization during economic recoveries, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in >60% of observations above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail and food services revenue reflects weakening consumer demand that forces equity analysts to cut forward earnings estimates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows no statistically significant difference in forward returns between triggered and non-triggered periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and accelerating CPI indicates a wage-price spiral that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1700 thousand units reflect strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as related sectors outperform.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where housing starts exceed the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI exceeds 285, elevated inflation combined with rising policy rates erodes real corporate margins and triggers risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the high inflation and rate signal.
